title: Briny Velvety Turkish Crème Patisserie Tart
description: A quick-fix Turkish dessert featuring crème patissiere, a briny glaze, and a silky smooth texture.
instructions: Make the pastry dough by mixing flour, sugar, and softened butter in a large bowl.
Add eggs and mix until a dough forms. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm). Transfer to a tart pan with a removable bottom.
Line the dough with parchment paper and fill with pie weights. Bake for 15 minutes.
Remove the parchment paper and pie weights. Return the tart shell to the oven and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, until lightly golden.
Make the crème patissiere by mixing heavy cream, granulated sugar, and unflavored gelatin in a medium saucepan.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and reaches 170°F (77°C).
Remove from heat and stir in softened butter and chopped fresh dill. Allow to cool to room temperature.
Strain the crème patissiere through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Cover with plastic wrap, pressing the wrap directly onto the surface of the crème.
Make the glaze by whisking together lemon juice and 1 tablespoon of water in a small bowl.
Spread the cooled crème patissiere into the baked tart shell. Drizzle with the glaze and serve.
